LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS SCM ADVISORS, LLC, VIRTUS INVESTMENT PARTNERS, INC., AND DIANE SPIRANDELLI'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS (TRANSFERRED FROM DEPT. 301, PER RECUSAL OF JUDGE BUSCH). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED. FAA APPEARS TO APPLY, AND THE LAST SENTENCE OF THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE DOES NOT INCLUDE ANY LANGUAGE THAT IS SPECIFIC ENOUGH TO JUSTIFY DISREGARDING THE FAA. MOTION ALSO GRANTED UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW. ALTHOUGH THE CLAIMS AGAINST THE CGMI AND THE SCM DEFENDANTS ARISE FROM THE SAME TRANSACTIONS, THE CLAIMS ARE QUITE DIFFERENT AND ANY PREJUDICE THAT MAY ARISE FROM CONSIDERING THE DISPUTES IN TWO SEPARATE FORUMS IS MINOR. CALIFORNIA LAW FAVORS ARBITRABILITY, AND THE RISK OF INCONSISTENT RULINGS IS SLIGHT. CGMI ACTION IS STAYED UNTIL THE SCM - FOUNDATION ARBITRATION IS COMPLETE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Mtn To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings; Appendix; Pos
Off Calendar Jan-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANTS S C M ADVISORS, LLC, VIRTUS INVESTMENT PARTNERS, INCORPORATED AND DIANE SPIRANDELLI'S MOTION To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings IS TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 3 0 2 PER JUDGE BUSCH'S RECUSAL. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

Defendants Scm Advisors, Llc, Virtus Investment Partners, Inc., And Diane Spirandelli'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings (Transferred From Dept. 301, Per Recusal Of Judge Busch)
Off Calendar Jan-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Defendants SCM Advisors, LLC, Virtus Investment Partners, Inc., and Diane Spirandelli's Motion To Compel Arbitration and Stay Proceedings (Transferred From Dept. 301, Per Recusal Of Judge Busch). Appearances: Aron K. Liang, Esq. of Cotchett, Pitre & McCarthy (650) 697-6000 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; J. Patrick Corrigan, Esq. & Gilbert R. Serota, Esq. of Howard Rice (415) 434-1600 appearing for Defendants and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted. FAA appears to apply, and the last sentence of the arbitration clause does not include any language that is specific enough to justify disregarding the FAA. Motion also granted under California law. Although the claims against the CGMI and the SCM defendants arise from the same transactions, the claims are quite different and any prejudice that may arise from considering the disputes in two separate forums is minor. California law favors arbitrability, and the risk of inconsistent rulings is slight. CGMI action is stayed until the SCM - Foundation arbitration is complete. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
